discussion_posts.name: amaroraiko
discussion_posts.content:
I found this problem to be really good. It has multiple levels of complexity and it's well explained with good examples. It is overall a good practice problem, and one you definitely don't want to skip if you're preparing for an interview.

At the time I'm writing this, the problem has 526 downvotes vs 195 upvotes. I looked around the comments and found no justification for the many downvotes, so I just think people downvote because they had a hard time solving it. Please **do not vote down only because you had a hard time solving a problem**.

This matters because this problem is really good for practicing, and people may not work on it thinking it's low quality (has failing tests, bad explanation, etc). I almost didn't work on it because of this reason.
